title: Add Two-Digit Numbers Within 100
description: Add two whole numbers within 100 to find a total.
condition: Given two whole numbers less than 100 to be combined.
response: Compute their sum accurately.
label: Principle

title: Compare Total to Target Amount
description: Determine whether a computed total is greater than, less than, or equal to a target amount.
condition: Given a computed total and a target amount (e.g., cost of an item).
response: State whether the total is enough (≥ target) or not.
label: Principle
example: Since 52 ≥ 50, Mai has enough money.

title: Convert Dollars to Cents
description: Use the equivalence that 1 dollar equals 100 cents.
condition: Given an amount in dollars (specifically $1).
response: State or use that $1 = 100$ cents.
label: Fact

title: Count Sides of a Polygon
description: Determine the number of straight sides in a closed shape.
condition: Given any closed polygon.
response: Count each straight side once and state the total number of sides.
label: Production / Skill
example: A shape with sides traced as 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 has a total of 5 sides.

title: Define Pentagon by Number of Sides
description: Understand that a pentagon is a polygon with 5 sides and 5 corners.
condition: Given a polygon with a known number of sides.
response: Classify it as a pentagon if and only if it has 5 sides.
label: Concept
example: If a shape has 5 sides and 5 vertices, then it is a pentagon.

title: Define Triangle by Number of Sides
description: Understand that a triangle is a polygon with 3 sides and 3 corners.
condition: Given a polygon or a drawing task requiring a triangle.
response: Identify or draw a shape with exactly 3 straight sides.
label: Concept
example: A shape with 3 sides is a triangle.
notes: Applies regardless of side lengths or angle measures.

title: Determine a.m. or p.m. from Context
description: Use contextual clues (morning, bedtime, etc.) to decide whether a time is a.m. or p.m.
condition: A described daily event (e.g., morning wake-up, going to bed).
response: Label the time as a.m. or p.m. appropriately.
label: Rule / Plan
example: If Jada gets up in the morning at 6:45, then it is 6:45 a.m.
notes: [merged: condition varies] [merged: response varies] [merged: label varies]

title: Draw Quadrilateral with Exactly Two Equal Sides
description: Create a four-sided figure in which exactly two sides are equal in length.
condition: Instruction to draw a quadrilateral with only two equal sides.
response: Draw a 4-sided closed figure where one pair of sides are equal and the other sides are different lengths.
label: Association
example: Draw a quadrilateral with side lengths 3 cm, 3 cm, 4 cm, and 5 cm.
notes: Classification as Association assumes task performance is primarily perceptual/constructive.???

title: Draw Triangle with Given Attributes
description: Construct a triangle that satisfies specified angle and side-length conditions.
condition: Given instructions to draw a triangle with 1 right (square) corner and 2 equal sides.
response: Draw a closed 3-sided figure with one 90° angle and two congruent sides.
label: Rule / Plan
example: Draw two perpendicular segments of length 4 units meeting at a right angle, then connect their endpoints to form a triangle with sides 4, 4, and $4\sqrt{2}$.

title: Equal Parts Define Fractional Partition
description: Recognize that a shape must be divided into equal-sized parts to represent halves, thirds, or fourths.
condition: A shape is partitioned into multiple parts and a fraction name (e.g., third, fourth) is referenced.
response: Determine whether all parts are equal in size.
label: Principle
example: If a rectangle is split into 3 parts and one part is larger than the others, then it is **not** divided into thirds because the parts are not equal.

title: Find Amount Needed to Reach 100
description: Subtract a current total from 100 to determine how much more is needed.
condition: Given a total amount less than 100 and goal of 100.
response: Compute $100 - \text{current total}$.
label: Principle / Model
example: If Jada has 42¢, then $100 - 42 = 58$ cents more are needed.

title: Find Missing Addend Using Subtraction
description: Use subtraction to find an unknown part when the total and one part are known.
condition: Given a total amount and one known part in a part–whole situation.
response: Subtract the known part from the total to find the unknown part.
label: Principle
example: If the total is 76 and one part is 39, compute 76 - 39 = 37.

title: Four Equal Parts Are Fourths
description: Identify that when a whole is divided into four equal parts, each part is called a fourth (or quarter).
condition: A shape is split into 4 equal parts.
response: State that each part is a fourth (or quarter).
label: Concept
example: If a square is divided into 4 equal regions, each region represents $\frac{1}{4}$ of the whole.

title: Identify Coin Value
description: Recall the value in cents of common U.S. coins (penny, nickel, dime, quarter).
condition: Given a specific U.S. coin (penny, nickel, dime, quarter).
response: State its value in cents (1, 5, 10, or 25).
label: Fact
example: A quarter is worth 25¢.
notes: Each coin-value pair is a separate fact but grouped here as a set of related facts.

title: Identify Equal Sides in a Triangle
description: Recognize or require that a triangle has two sides of equal length.
condition: Given a triangle or a requirement that two sides have the same length.
response: State or construct a triangle with at least two congruent sides.
label: Concept
example: A triangle with side lengths 3, 3, and 4 units has two equal sides.
notes: In Grade 2, described as '2 sides that have the same length.'

title: Identify Hour from Hour Hand Position
description: Determine the hour by locating where the short hand points or has most recently passed.
condition: An analog clock with a visible hour hand position.
response: State the hour number the short hand is pointing to or has just passed.
label: Production / Skill
example: If the short hand is between 2 and 3 (closer to 3), the hour is $2$ (as in $2{:}45$).
notes: Students must distinguish the hour hand from the minute hand.

title: Interpret “Quarter Past”
description: Translate the phrase “quarter past” into 15 minutes after the stated hour.
condition: A time phrase of the form "quarter past h."
response: Convert to digital time $h{:}15$.
label: Rule / Plan
example: "Quarter past 2" corresponds to $2{:}15$.

title: One of Three Equal Parts Is One Third
description: Determine that shading exactly one of three equal parts represents one third of the whole.
condition: A shape is divided into 3 equal parts and exactly 1 part is shaded.
response: State that $\frac{1}{3}$ (one third) of the shape is shaded.
label: Principle
example: If 1 of 3 equal sections is shaded, then the shaded portion is $\frac{1}{3}$.

title: Partition Rectangle into Four Equal Parts
description: Create four equal regions within a rectangle.
condition: Given a rectangle and instructed to split it into 4 equal parts.
response: Draw partition lines that create 4 equal-sized regions.
label: Category
example: Draw three evenly spaced vertical lines to create 4 equal strips in the rectangle.
notes: Multiple correct partitions possible (vertical, horizontal, grid).

title: Recognize a Right (Square) Corner
description: Identify or construct a 90° angle, often called a square corner.
condition: Given an angle in a shape or a requirement for a square corner.
response: Identify or create an angle measuring 90°.
label: Concept
example: An angle formed by perpendicular segments (like the corner of a square) is a right angle (90°).
notes: In Grade 2 language, referred to as a 'square corner.'

title: Sum Values of Multiple Coins
description: Multiply or repeatedly add coin values based on quantity and combine totals.
condition: Given quantities of different coin types.
response: Calculate the total value in cents by combining each coin's value times its quantity.
label: Principle
example: 3 pennies (3) + 4 nickels (4×5=20) + 1 dime (10) + 2 quarters (2×25=50) gives 3 + 20 + 10 + 50 = 83.

title: Whole as Sum of Equal Fractional Parts
description: Recognize that when all equal fractional parts are shaded, they compose one whole.
condition: All equal parts of a partitioned shape (e.g., 4 fourths) are shaded.
response: State that the entire shape (one whole) is shaded.
label: Principle
example: If all 4 parts of a square divided into fourths are shaded, then $4 \times \frac{1}{4} = 1$ whole.
notes: Connects repeated unit fractions to 1 whole.

title: Write Amount Using Dollar Symbol
description: Express a money amount using the dollar symbol correctly.
condition: Given a whole-number dollar amount.
response: Write the amount with the "$" symbol preceding the number.
label: Rule
example: Write 37 dollars as $37.
